title: Sequences of Real Numbers
description: Module 1 — From the ε–N definition to Cauchy completeness

nodes.label: Limit Point of a Set\n\((x-\varepsilon, x+\varepsilon) \cap A \setminus\{x\} \ne \emptyset\)
Order Limit Theorem\n\(a_n \le b_n \Rightarrow \lim a_n \le \lim b_n\)

edges.label: for \(a_n = ar^{n-1}\) (nonzero terms), \(a_{n+1}/a_n =\) ___
edges.hint: Compute the ratio of consecutive terms and simplify.

edges.label: the statement \"\(\forall \varepsilon>0\,\exists N\,\forall n>N:\ |a_n-L|<\varepsilon\)\" defines ___
edges.hint: Name the concept encoded by the epsilon-N quantifiers.

edges.label: if no real \(L\) satisfies epsilon-N convergence, the sequence ___
edges.hint: Use the standard term opposite to convergence.

edges.label: \(x\) is a limit point of \(A\) if every punctured neighborhood of \(x\) meets \(A\) in ___ many points
edges.hint: Finite exceptions are not enough for this definition.

edges.label: a convergent real sequence has exactly ___ limit
edges.hint: Uniqueness is a theorem consequence of the definition.

edges.label: \(\lim(a_n+b_n)=\lim a_n+\lim b_n\) is the ___ rule
edges.hint: Identify the operation preserved under limits.

edges.label: if \(b_n \le a_n \le c_n\) and both outer limits are \(L\), then \(a_n \to\) ___
edges.hint: The middle sequence is trapped by two sequences with equal limit.

edges.label: if \(a_n \le b_n\) for all \(n\), then \(\lim a_n\) ___ \(\lim b_n\)
edges.hint: In general, limits preserve weak order.

edges.label: removing finitely many initial terms ___ the limit
edges.hint: Convergence depends on tail behavior.

edges.label: every convergent real sequence is ___
edges.hint: There exists a global bound \(M\) for all terms.

edges.label: bounded means \(\exists M\) with \(|a_n|\le\) ___ for all \(n\)
edges.hint: Fill in the bound constant.

edges.label: if terms are always nondecreasing or nonincreasing, the sequence is ___
edges.hint: Use the standard one-word property.

edges.label: the proof of MCT uses a least upper ___ coming from completeness
edges.hint: Think LUB: the candidate value is the supremum of the set of terms.

edges.label: boundedness alone is not enough; MCT also needs the sequence to be ___
edges.hint: A bounded oscillating sequence shows boundedness by itself does not force convergence.

edges.label: monotonicity alone is not enough; MCT also needs the sequence to be ___
edges.hint: An increasing sequence like \(a_n=n\) shows why this extra hypothesis is needed.

edges.label: for Babylonian iteration, AM-GM gives \(x_{n+1}\ge\) ___
edges.hint: Apply AM-GM to \(x_n\) and \(c/x_n\).

edges.label: to use MCT for a decreasing sequence, it must be bounded ___
edges.hint: For decreasing sequences, a lower bound is the useful one.

edges.label: the arithmetic and geometric mean iterates converge to the same ___
edges.hint: Their gap shrinks to zero while order is preserved.

edges.label: a subsequence uses strictly ___ indices \(n_1<n_2<\cdots\)
edges.hint: Indices must move forward through \(\mathbb{N}\).

edges.label: a sequence can have ___ subsequential limits
edges.hint: Oscillating sequences can have more than one cluster value.

edges.label: \(\{a_n\}\) converges iff even and odd subsequences have the ___ limit
edges.hint: Different even/odd limits force divergence.

edges.label: if \(a_n\to L\), then the Cesaro means have the same ___
edges.hint: Averaging preserves the limit in this direction.

edges.label: an index with no later term exceeding \(a_n\) is a ___ point
edges.hint: Think skyline maxima while scanning forward.

edges.label: peak-point dichotomy implies every sequence has a ___ subsequence
edges.hint: This is exactly the monotone subsequence lemma.

edges.label: to prove BW from monotone subsequences, combine boundedness with ___
edges.hint: Use the theorem that converts bounded monotone sequences into convergent ones.

edges.label: BW states: every ___ sequence in \(\mathbb{R}\) has a convergent subsequence
edges.hint: Only one hypothesis appears in the theorem statement.

edges.label: if \(a_n\to L\), then \(|a_m-a_n|\) is eventually ___
edges.hint: Use triangle inequality through the common limit \(L\).

edges.label: with \(\varepsilon=1\), Cauchy tails lie in an interval of length ___
edges.hint: Radius 1 gives diameter 2.

edges.label: from boundedness, BW gives a ___ subsequence
edges.hint: Name the guaranteed subsequence property.

edges.label: if \(a_{n_k}\to L\) and \(\{a_n\}\) is Cauchy, then \(a_n\to\) ___
edges.hint: Compare \(a_n\) to \(a_{n_k}\), then \(a_{n_k}\) to \(L\).

edges.label: in \(\mathbb{R}\), Cauchy \(\iff\) ___
edges.hint: This is the Cauchy convergence criterion in a complete space.
